title: Cloud-native news chatbot
summary: Real-time news summarization and conversational discovery for one of Italy's major digital outlets. Multi-agent architecture with persistent memory, streaming responses, and semantic retrieval over a live editorial corpus.
description: Conversational layer on top of the publisher's editorial content. The bot can summarize articles, compare topics, recommend related reads, and surface trending stories — all grounded in the live corpus with explicit citations. Multi-agent orchestration: a coordinator dispatches to specialized retrieval, generation, and trending-detection agents.
extra_context: Built end-to-end as the lead engineer. The hardest part was retrieval quality: the system was rewritten to use semantic chunking by editorial unit (lede, body sections, pull quotes) instead of fixed token windows, which improved grounding significantly. Hard refusal policy on empty retrieval — the bot won't fall back to general world knowledge. Streaming responses via SSE. Architecture is intentionally GCP-native (Cloud Run + Firestore + Gemini) to keep the operational surface simple.

title: AI news ingestion & RAG platform
summary: Multi-tenant SaaS that ingests news from arbitrary RSS and JSON feeds, enriches them with topic extraction and metadata, and exposes a conversational layer that dynamically chooses between tool-use and RAG.
description: The platform pulls news feeds from clients' configured sources, runs enrichment (topic classification, entity extraction, metadata), stores articles and vector embeddings on S3, and exposes them through an agent that intelligently picks the right retrieval strategy per question. Supports multiple LLM providers but defaults to Bedrock so client data stays within AWS.

metrics.label: articles ingested per day across tenants

extra_context: Monorepo architecture in TypeScript. Hybrid agent design: the LLM decides per query whether to use structured tool calls (filter by date, topic, source) or semantic RAG retrieval, sometimes combining both. Infrastructure fully managed via Terraform. Data residency was a hard requirement — Bedrock keeps everything inside the client's AWS account. Currently in delivery, not yet GA with end customers but operationally complete.

title: Document AI for expiration tracking
summary: Backend AI service for a Flutter mobile app that extracts expiration dates from photos of documents (receipts, medical prescriptions, ID cards), categorizes them, and syncs the result to the user's calendar.
description: Vision LLM pipeline that takes a user photo, identifies the document type, extracts the relevant expiration date and metadata, and pushes it back to the mobile client which writes it into the device calendar. Privacy-first design — all inference happens within AWS via Bedrock, no data leaves the client's perimeter.
extra_context: Backend-only role — the Flutter app is built by the client team. The interesting part was building a vision pipeline that gracefully handles document quality variance (blurry photos, weird angles, partial occlusion) and gives the LLM enough structured prompting to extract the date even from documents where the expiration field isn't standardized. Designed for privacy: Bedrock means data residency stays in the client's AWS account.

title: LorenzoBot
summary: Cloud-native AI assistant integrated into lorenzomaiuri.dev. Provides accurate, context-aware information about Lorenzo's work, stack, and availability using multi-agent reasoning, persistent memory, and Gemini orchestration.
description: The chatbot you're talking to right now. Multi-agent system: a router classifies the intent, then dispatches to one of four specialists (project agent, technical agent, availability agent, contact agent). Streaming SSE responses, structured citations that link to relevant pages of this site, and action triggers that can open the contact modal or navigate to a specific case study.
extra_context: Self-referential project — Lorenzo built the bot that explains his work. The architecture deliberately mirrors the production news chatbot for a major IT outlet, scaled down to portfolio scope. Cloud Run scale-to-zero means the bot costs ~zero when idle. Open source under AGPL-3.0 at github.com/lorenzomaiuri-dev/lorenzo-maiuri-chatbot.

title: BERT GDPR classifier
summary: Fine-tuned BERT for personal data detection in order notes, deployed into a B2B production platform for GDPR compliance workflows.
description: Replaced a semi-manual GDPR review process with an automated NLP pipeline. Fine-tuned BERT identifies personal references (names, identifiers, addresses) in free-text order notes and flags them for review. Integrated into the company's existing .NET stack.
extra_context: Production deployment integrated with the existing compliance workflow. The model is retrainable as the data distribution evolves. Notable because it's a real ML system shipping into a regulated industry, not a notebook prototype.

title: Data warehouse modernization
summary: Oracle to PostgreSQL migration with full ETL rewrite on Airflow and CI/CD pipeline. Restructured the warehouse model and documented downstream consumers throughout the transition.
description: End-to-end migration of the company's data warehouse from Oracle to PostgreSQL. Redesigned ETL pipelines on Apache Airflow, set up CI/CD for SQL transformations, and ensured zero data loss during cutover. Modernized the analytics stack and removed Oracle licensing costs.
extra_context: Multi-month engagement. The interesting part wasn't the SQL translation — it was managing dozens of downstream consumers (reports, dashboards, internal tools) and coordinating their cutover without disrupting business operations.

title: Logistics anomaly dashboard
summary: Real-time cost control dashboard for logistics operations with ML-based anomaly detection on shipment data. Surfaces outliers and pricing irregularities automatically.
description: Built a dashboard that processes courier invoices, cross-references them with internal shipment data, and uses anomaly detection to flag discrepancies between invoiced amounts and actual deliveries. Saved finance team significant manual reconciliation work.
extra_context: Combined data engineering + ML in a single deliverable. The anomaly detection model was intentionally simple (statistical outliers + a few business rules) — over-engineering with deep learning would have been waste.

title: Order prioritization engine
summary: ML-based prioritization system integrated into a B2B platform. Ranks incoming orders by urgency and downstream impact, accelerating processing for time-critical requests.
description: Worked with data scientists to integrate a machine learning prioritization model into the existing B2B platform. The model predicts order urgency based on multiple business factors and re-ranks the operator queue in real-time.

title: Histopathologic cancer CNN
summary: Deep learning classifier for metastatic tissue detection in lymph node histopathology scans. Public on Hugging Face Hub.
description: CNN-based binary classifier built on the Kaggle Histopathologic Cancer Detection dataset. Takes small patches of pathology images and predicts whether the center 32x32 region contains tumor tissue.

title: DanteGPT
summary: GPT-2 fine-tuned on the Divina Commedia to generate text in Dante Alighieri's poetic style, including tercet structure, ABA BCB CDC rhyme scheme, and thematic elements.
description: Personal NLP project. Fine-tuned GPT-2 on the full text of the Divina Commedia, with the model learning to emulate Dante's structural and stylistic conventions. Available on Hugging Face Hub.

title: Misogyny detection in Italian text
summary: Fine-tuned BERT for binary classification of misogynistic content in Italian text. Trained and evaluated on the AMI dataset.
description: NLP research project on Italian-language hate speech detection. Fine-tuned BERT on the AMI (Automatic Misogyny Identification) dataset. Available on Hugging Face Hub.

title: SmolAgent on GAIA benchmark
summary: Custom multimodal agent built on the SmolAgent framework, evaluated on the GAIA benchmark. Earned a Certificate of Excellence from the Hugging Face Agents Course.
description: End-to-end project from the Hugging Face Agents Course. Built a multimodal agent capable of solving complex real-world tasks through tool orchestration, reasoning via code, and multimodal understanding. Achieved Certificate of Excellence.

title: AI customer support chatbot
summary: End-to-end AI chatbot for customer support, answering from diverse company documentation. Integrated with Salesforce and a WordPress front-end.
description: RAG-based customer support assistant for a global audio company. Indexed company documentation, integrated with Salesforce for case escalation, and embedded into the WordPress storefront. Observability via Phoenix by Arize.
extra_context: Not promoted on the public portfolio, but ask Lorenzo about it if relevant. Earlier engagement that informed the architecture of later projects.

title: Apache Airflow migration
summary: Migration from a custom middleware to Apache Airflow, resolving performance bottlenecks and improving reliability.
description: Replaced an in-house middleware orchestrator with Apache Airflow. Redesigned workflows, optimized code, and ensured a smooth cutover.
